NADP_trend_analysis

Bella Oleksy

2024-11-19

Some notes at the top about data processing

Interpolation method– - Here, I am linearlly interpolated for periods of missing data with gaps no more than 2 weeks apart. I am using the NO3, NH4, Total IN, and precip values from Bret A. Schichtel et al. 2019 Sci Tot Env.

Year vs. water year– Aggregating data on an annual basis instead of WY. Just flagging that in case we switch to WY later.

Missing data:

Data are missing throughout, unsurprisingly, but there are a few time periods that stick out, like 1986. I will exclude them for the initial trend analysis because they are biased low.

Test for annual deposition trends

Demo of what’s happening under the hood – run the M-K test, and if significant extract the Sens slope and intercept.

## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  N_dep_annual$deposition_kg_per_ha_impute
## W = 0.88693, p-value = 0.0009506
## 
##  Mann-Kendall trend test
## 
## data:  N_dep_annual$deposition_kg_per_ha_impute
## z = -0.16936, n = 39, p-value = 0.8655
## alternative hypothesis: true S is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##             S          varS           tau 
##  -15.00000000 6833.66666667   -0.02024291
## 
##  Sen's slope
## 
## data:  N_dep_annual$deposition_kg_per_ha_impute
## z = -0.16936, n = 39, p-value = 0.8655
## alternative hypothesis: true z is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.01812566  0.01550202
## sample estimates:
##   Sen's slope 
## -0.0008530628
## 
## Call:
## NULL
## 
## Coefficients:
##  Intercept   waterYear  
##  4.6202497  -0.0008531
